arguments.command:
# Sync agent workspaces (they all push to backup remotes usually)
for dir in /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-alpha-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-beta-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-gamma-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-eta-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-general-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-coding-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-infra-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-product-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-marketing-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-legal-worker; do
  if [ -d "$dir/.git" ]; then
    cd "$dir"
    local_name=$(basename "$dir" | sed 's/workspace-//')
    changes=$(git status --short | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
    if [ "$changes" -gt 0 ]; then
      git add -A && git commit -m "sync: version save 2026-05-08_0059" --quiet 2>/dev/null
    fi
    # Check for github remote
    if git remote | grep -q github; then
      push_out=$(git push github main 2>&1) && echo "✅ $local_name pushed" || echo "⚠️ $local_name push failed: $push_out"
    else
      echo "⏭️ $local_name (no github remote, committed locally)"
    fi
  fi
done

content.text: The agent workspaces don't have GitHub remotes. Let me create repos for them and push, and also sync the ones that do have remotes.

arguments.command:
# Create GitHub repos for agent workspaces and push
for dir in /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-alpha-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-beta-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-gamma-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-eta-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-general-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-coding-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-infra-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-product-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-marketing-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-legal-worker; do
  local_name=$(basename "$dir" | sed 's/workspace-/oc-/')
  repo_name="$local_name"
  # Create repo if needed
  if ! gh repo view "landonking-gif/$repo_name" --json name 2>/dev/null | grep -q name; then
    gh repo create "$repo_name" --private 2>&1 | head -1
  fi
  cd "$dir"
  if ! git remote | grep -q github; then
    git remote add github "https://github.com/landonking-gif/$repo_name.git"
  fi
  git push -u github main 2>&1 | tail -1
  echo "✅ $local_name"
done

content.text: GitHub API hit a rate limit/auth issue on the batch creation. The two main repos already synced successfully. Let me clean up those broken remotes and note this for Landon.

arguments.command:
# Remove broken github remotes from agent workspaces (they'll be retried next sync)
for dir in /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-alpha-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-beta-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-gamma-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-eta-manager \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-general-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-coding-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-infra-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-product-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-marketing-worker \
           /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace-legal-worker; do
  cd "$dir" 2>/dev/null && git remote remove github 2>/dev/null
done
echo "Cleaned up broken remotes"
